signals.title: Astranis Surpasses All Other Customers in WAU — Quietly Becoming Your Power User
signals.detail: Astranis (Dev + Mission combined) hit 235 WAU last week — surpassing Sift internal (114) and every other customer. They grew from 195→235 in 4 weeks while Sift internal declined 129→114. Astranis is now 39% of your external WAU. Their engagement is deepening (Family Data onboarding 3 teams, ABAC enablement underway) but they're blocked by data access governance issues (ENG-8732, ENG-8721, ENG-8727). If ABAC slips, your largest customer's expansion stalls.
signals.action: In your Kenn 1:1 Thursday: confirm DAG V1 PRs are coordinated with Astranis activation timeline. Ask Sanghee in today's Product Sync about the data leak tickets she flagged — are they prerequisites for ABAC rollout?

signals.title: Lockheed Resourcing Meeting Today Signals Scope Expansion — Global Nav Is Now Two Projects
signals.detail: Kenn's 2pm meeting description reveals Lockheed feedback has split the original Global Nav work into two separate scopes: Resource/Folder Management AND Global Nav improvements. Combined with Gui's deployment update showing an action plan to 'address product areas inhibiting adoption' and 'execute on Artemis II launch criteria,' Lockheed is becoming a forcing function for GNA scope. Thursday's GNA workshop (11:15am-2pm, 3-hour block with Russ) and tomorrow's UX Research Readout on tree testing are the moments to set boundaries.
signals.action: At the 2pm Lockheed Resourcing call: push for clear priority stack between Resource/Folder Management vs Global Nav. Don't let Lockheed scope creep absorb the GNA workshop Thursday. Use the tree test readout tomorrow (4pm) to anchor decisions in data, not customer pressure.

signals.title: Telemetry Debt Is Real — Sanghee Flagged It, and Your Rule Events Confirm It
signals.detail: Three separate signals converging: (1) Sanghee messaged this morning that 'some teams were not quite up to date when it comes to event instrumentation' and wants to discuss telemetry debt before next planning, (2) the Key Actions Volume chart shows create/save/edit rule clicks all at ZERO for 12 weeks — clearly broken instrumentation, not zero usage since Rules WAU is at 70 and growing, (3) you and Kenn couldn't open each other's Amplitude links Sunday, suggesting permissions fragmentation. Your North Star metric 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' literally cannot be measured if telemetry is broken.
signals.action: Greenlight Sanghee's request to audit instrumentation with Brandon. This should be a prerequisite gate before Q2 planning. Raise in Product Sync today (2:30pm): propose a 1-week telemetry sprint across squads to fix event gaps.

signals.title: DAU Hit 373 Monday — Highest Single Day in 30-Day Window. Something Changed.
signals.detail: Monday March 2 DAU spiked to 373 — the highest in the 30-day window, up from prior Monday highs of 347 (2/24) and 321 (2/17). WAU is stable at 598 but this daily concentration suggests more users are active on Mondays specifically. Session duration continues climbing: 116 min/user/week, now the highest since early December. Users are spending almost 2 hours per week in Sift. Combined with Explore hitting a record 387 WAU, the platform is becoming stickier without new user growth driving it.
signals.action: Use this in your All Hands presentation Thursday: 'Users are spending 2 hours/week in Sift — that's not checking a dashboard, that's doing real engineering work inside our platform.' The depth story is stronger than the breadth story right now.

signals.title: K2 Space Is Stress-Testing Your Live Ops Architecture — and Finding the Ceiling
signals.detail: K2 filed 4 tickets last week (3 High priority) all pointing at the same gap: Sift can't serve real-time mission infrastructure. They need low-latency fresh data reads, telemetry absence detection, enriched webhook payloads, and stable live mode. Gui's deployment update confirms 'K2 continues to stress Sift with very high-volume operations, exposing E2 live-mode UX bugs.' Meanwhile DFS team is building exactly this capability: DLV3 Fresh Data Reads is on track, CDS hit 4.9M points/sec. The supply and demand exist — they're just not connected yet.
signals.action: In Product Review Thursday (1:30pm): draw explicit line between K2 live ops tickets and DFS/CDS progress. Ask: when does fresh-data-reads reach K2? If the answer is 'Q2,' communicate the timeline to K2 via Gui to hold the relationship.

signals.title: Canvas MVP Timeline at Risk — Varda SLA Absorbed the Eng Team for 2-4 Weeks
signals.detail: Your Product Bets framework has Canvas at the Design/Prototype gate needing 2-of-3 evidence types to advance. Prototype validation happened (5 users across ULA + Astro Mechanica), reports can now be published/shared, parameterization in PR. But: (1) Brandon's squad is splitting time on Varda SLA operational improvements, (2) Sanghee can't get a test environment — she was exploring Vercel but pivoted to waiting for ephemeral environments (Jared says 'next week'), (3) all Canvas eng is on Varda SLA per Sanghee. The kill criteria clock is ticking.
signals.action: In Sanghee 1:1 Thursday (2:30pm): assess Canvas velocity honestly. Is ephemeral env actually landing this week? Should you invoke the kill criteria conversation, or does the prototype validation evidence justify continued investment despite slower velocity?

signals.title: Rules Adoption Surge Is Outrunning Instrumentation and Product Quality
signals.detail: Rules WAU jumped from 48→70 (+46%) in 2 weeks — the fastest-growing feature area. But customers are already outgrowing it: backfill-aware re-evaluation (SCS-1119, High), rule-to-annotation quick-plot (SCS-1125), email group notifications (SCS-1116). Python Rules is pausing after alpha hardening. Meanwhile your rule event telemetry shows zero clicks across create/save/edit for 12 straight weeks, meaning you're flying blind on actual usage patterns during the biggest adoption surge.
signals.action: Prioritize rule telemetry fix as part of the instrumentation sprint. For the Review Top Customer Asks meeting today (3:30pm): stack-rank the 3 rules tickets by customer tier impact. SCS-1119 (backfill re-eval) is likely the highest-leverage fix.

signals.title: New User Retention Day 1-7 Is ~22% — Your Growth Bottleneck Is Activation, Not Acquisition
signals.detail: 89 new users joined last week (consistent ~90/week run rate). But bracket retention shows: Day 0→1 is 100% (good), Day 1-7 drops to ~22%, Day 7-14 to ~15%, Day 14-30 to ~10%. You're acquiring users but 78% disappear within a week. Given Sift's hardware test cycle patterns, some of this is structural. But OHB authentication issues blocking invite redemption and ImpulseSpace gRPC SSL corruption suggest real activation barriers exist for new deployments.
signals.action: For your biweekly product update (due 3/16): frame retention as the Q2 theme. 'We've proven value depth (2hr sessions) — now we need to prove value breadth (activation rate).' Flag OHB and Impulse auth blockers to Rowen in your Thursday 1:1.

signals.title: VP of Engineering Starts March 24 — You Have 3 Weeks to Shape the Product-Eng Interface
signals.detail: Mike Russell starts as VP of Engineering on 3/24. Talent update also shows VP of Sales in final interviews (Jimmy Morocco, Ben Schmidt). The leadership team is about to change shape. Your product operating rhythm — the weekly health reports, biweekly updates, Product Bets framework, rituals calendar — is infrastructure that a new VPE will either adopt or work around. The next 3 weeks are your window to solidify these systems so they become organizational defaults, not just Noah's tools.
signals.action: Before 3/24: ensure the Amplitude dashboard, Linear roadmap, and product rituals calendar are documented and shared broadly enough that they survive a leadership transition. Consider scheduling an onboarding session with Mike Russell in his first week to establish the product-eng operating contract.

signals.title: Kenn's Linear Label Taxonomy Push — Small Move, Big Downstream Impact
signals.detail: Kenn posted today that he's adding short descriptions to Linear area/feature labels, starting with Ingestion. This seems small but is actually infrastructure for your North Star metric: if labels are inconsistent, you can't accurately map customer requests to product areas, which means your 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' metric has noisy denominators. Kenn noted he 'still gets confused on what goes where' — if your PM can't triage correctly, customers definitely can't.
signals.action: Amplify this in Product Sync today: make it a shared PM team exercise, not just Kenn's solo project. Each PM should own their areas' label descriptions by end of week. This directly enables the customer intelligence layer you've been building.

signals.title: Product Bets Framework Announced — All Hands Thursday Is Your Strategy Moment
signals.detail: You posted the Product Bets Framework & Pipeline to #product this morning, flagging it will be presented at All Hands (Thursday 10:15am). This is your first company-wide strategy presentation with the three-gate system (Design/Prototype → Investment/Ship MVP → Scale/Pack). Canvas, Manufacturing/Live Ops, and Robotics bets all have kill criteria. With Squad Showcase at 3pm Thursday, you're bookending the day with strategy + execution.
signals.action: For All Hands: lead with the 2-hour session depth stat as proof of product-market fit, then present the bets framework as 'how we decide what to build next.' Keep it to 5 minutes — the framework's power is in its simplicity. Save the kill criteria details for product team only.

signals.title: Share Link Views at 12-Week High (244) — Your Users Are Selling Sift for You
signals.detail: Chart share link views hit 244 last week — the highest in 12 weeks. Chart share icon clicks at 119 and annotation creates at 13. The ratio of views to shares (244:119 = 2.05x) means each shared chart is viewed by ~2 people, indicating organic spread within customer organizations. This is your best leading indicator for seat expansion and 'Embed Deeper' strategy pillar success.
signals.action: Track share-to-view ratio as a formal metric. If each share generates 2+ views, your collaboration features are working as a growth loop. Mention in the biweekly update: 'Every chart shared reaches 2 additional viewers — our users are our best salesforce.'

signals.title: Thursday Is Your Heaviest Day — 8 Meetings Including GNA Workshop, All Hands, Product Review
signals.detail: Thursday March 5 schedule: Kenn 1:1 (9:30), All Hands (10:15), GNA Standup (11:00), GNA Workshop (11:15-2:00), Rowen 1:1 (1:00 — overlaps workshop), Product Review (1:30), Sanghee 1:1 (2:30), Squad Showcase (3:00). The GNA Workshop is a 2h45m block that conflicts with your Rowen 1:1 and Product Review. This is a triage problem.
signals.action: Move Rowen 1:1 to Wednesday or skip and async. GNA Workshop is where real design decisions happen — you need to be there for at least the first 90 min to set direction, then hand off to Russ/Clint. Protect Product Review at 1:30 — that's your cross-squad alignment moment.

signals.title: CVE Remediation Remains the Silent Blocker — ULA and Chainguard Timeline Risk
signals.detail: Jared's project update marks CVE remediation as 'At Risk' — the only yellow project in the admin-weekly-notes. They're purchasing Chainguard for CVE-free FIPS images but it may not be ready in time for ULA. A contingency plan is being built. Meanwhile ULA filed compliance tickets (classification banners, dev/prod indicators) that signal they're evaluating Sift's security posture holistically. If CVE fix + compliance UX don't converge, ULA activation stays blocked indefinitely.
signals.action: Escalate to Austin/Karthik: CVE remediation timeline and Chainguard contract status need executive attention. This is the single biggest customer revenue blocker and it's an infrastructure problem, not a product problem. Your role: ensure product-side compliance features (SCS-1123, SCS-1122) are prioritized as the 'easy win' while infra catches up.

signals.title: Sift Edge Backend Prototype Complete — Momentum Building Before Demo Day
signals.detail: Benji's SIK update: Edge backend prototype can now serve reads and writes, frontend prototyping has begun, sync-to-Sift functionality is underway. RFC will be shared broadly next week. They're targeting Squad Showcase for a demo. This is one of your five AI product bets and it's moving faster than Canvas right now despite less attention.
signals.action: In Squad Lead Sync Friday (10:30am): ask Benji for Edge demo timeline. If it's ready for Showcase Thursday, amplify it at All Hands as a concrete example of the bets framework in action: 'Here's a bet moving through Gate 1.'

decisions.title: Canvas: continue to MVP or kill
decisions.context: Canvas prototype validated in user testing but eng velocity stalled. Varda SLA absorbed 2-4 weeks of eng capacity. Ephemeral env delayed. Kill criteria from Product Bets framework: need 2-of-3 evidence types to advance past Gate 1.
decisions.evidenceAgainst: Eng absorbed by Varda, velocity stalled, no ephemeral env

decisions.title: Lockheed GNA: accept scope split or push back
decisions.context: Lockheed split Global Nav into Resource/Folder Management + Global Nav improvements. Combined with Gui's 'product areas inhibiting adoption' note. Thursday's GNA workshop (3 hours) and tree test readout are decision windows.
decisions.evidenceFor: Customer is investing in the product, expanding usage
decisions.evidenceAgainst: Scope creep, two projects instead of one, resource drain

decisions.title: Varda SLA: dedicated staffing or time-box
decisions.context: Varda SLA commitments absorbing eng team 2-4 weeks. Blocking Canvas. WAU only 32 (5% of external). Is the eng cost proportionate?
decisions.evidenceAgainst: Disproportionate eng cost, blocking higher-leverage Canvas work

decisions.title: VPE onboarding: what to institutionalize before 3/24
decisions.context: VP Engineering starts 3/24. Three-week window to solidify product operating systems (rituals, 4P cadence, product-eng interface) as org defaults before new eng leader arrives.
decisions.evidenceFor: Once VPE arrives, changing process becomes political. Set defaults now.
decisions.evidenceAgainst: Risk of over-prescribing before understanding new VPE's style

decisions.title: Telemetry instrumentation: urgent fix or roadmap item
decisions.context: Rule events showing ZERO for 12 weeks despite Rules WAU at 70 and growing +46% WoW. North Star metric (Decisions Captured Per Customer) literally cannot be measured. Sanghee flagged this morning.
decisions.evidenceFor: Flying blind during biggest adoption surge. Must fix now.
decisions.evidenceAgainst: Instrumentation work competes with feature delivery

themes.name: Data Access & Governance

evidence.label: ENG-8732: ABAC for Astranis Family Data

themes.name: Live Operations Reliability
themes.summary: Real-time reads, telemetry absence detection, enriched webhooks, live mode stability. K2 has 4 high-priority tickets. Varda SLA requiring 2-4 weeks eng investment.

themes.name: Navigation & Discoverability
themes.summary: Global nav redesign, resource management, folder hierarchy. Lockheed split GNA into two projects. Gui flagged 'product areas inhibiting adoption.' Tree test readout this week.

themes.name: Ingestion & Data Quality
themes.summary: Upload failures, file type expansion needs, Python client adoption. Upload UX refresh in progress. Python client is Q1 priority for Clients+Ingest squad.

themes.name: Reporting & Decision Capture
themes.summary: Report versioning, collaborative review workflows, export formats. Ties directly to North Star metric (Decisions Captured Per Customer). Canvas bet addresses this but velocity stalled.

themes.name: Performance & Scale
themes.summary: P99 read query latency, large dataset handling, Explore performance under load. Rust data layer migration in progress to address fundamentally.

events.context: Rule events showing ZERO in Amplitude for 12 weeks. North Star metric literally unmeasurable.

events.title: Canvas Kill/Continue Gate

events.context: Document and systematize the eng onboarding process before it becomes tribal knowledge.

events.context: E2 currently in beta. GA targeting mid-March. ECharts replacement scoped for Q2.

events.context: Last showcase of Q1. All squads expected to demo shipped work.

events.context: Internal tree test with FDE and Deployment team. Findings inform Global Nav Redesign next steps.

events.title: GNA Workshop (2.75hr)
events.context: Review designs, discuss UI patterns for navigation and sitemap architecture. Bring lunch, casual.

events.title: Astranis ABAC Escalation Window
events.context: 3 Astranis teams waiting on ABAC for Family Data. Each week of delay = blocked expansion revenue.

events.title: K2 Space Live Ops Checkpoint
events.context: K2 stress-testing E2 live-mode. 4 high-priority live ops tickets open. Real-time reads critical.

events.title: Varda SLA Delivery Window
events.context: SLA requiring guaranteed uptime and data freshness. 2-4 weeks eng investment committed.

events.title: Heart Aerospace Re-engagement
events.context: WAU declining (-4 in 4 weeks). FDE touch 11d ago. Going quiet. Need proactive outreach before Q1 end.

events.title: ULA Activation Target

events.title: Weekly Customer Intelligence Report

events.title: Monthly Roadmap Review
events.context: Monthly cadence. Review roadmap priorities against customer asks and strategy.

events.context: Pull 4P docs, query Amplitude, synthesize, post to #product. Team 4Ps due Fri EOD prior.

events.title: Strategy Alignment Workshop
events.context: Q2 planning Week 2. Align on company priorities, market positioning, competitive landscape.

events.title: OKR Drafting Session
events.context: Q2 planning Week 3. Draft Q2 OKRs based on strategy alignment outputs.

events.title: Roadmap Prioritization
events.context: Q2 planning Week 4. Stack-rank initiatives against OKRs. Resource allocation.

events.title: Q2 Kickoff
events.context: Q2 planning Week 5. Final ceremony. Communicate plan to org. Squads begin execution.
